mathematical_rationale: The revised CutAndPaste property states that if two input pairs $(x_1, y_1)$ and $(x_2, y_2)$ generate the same crossing sequence, then swapping the $Y$-components preserves the output: $\text{Out}(x_1, y_2) = \text{Out}(x_1, y_1)$. In a fooling set $S$, $\text{Out}$ is true on diagonal pairs and false on off-diagonal pairs when the $X$-components differ. The additional hypothesis $\forall p_1, p_2 \in S,\, p_1.1 = p_2.1 \to p_1 = p_2$ ensures that $S$ contains at most one pair per $X$-value. Under these conditions, if two distinct pairs in $S$ had the same crossing sequence, their $X$-components must differ (by the diagonal uniqueness). The fooling property guarantees $\text{Out}(p_1.1, p_2.2) = \text{false}$, but CutAndPaste forces $\text{Out}(p_1.1, p_2.2) = \text{Out}(p_1.1, p_1.2) = \text{true}$, a contradiction. Hence $\text{CS}$ is injective on $S$, yielding a lower bound on the size of the crossing sequence alphabet.
lean_4_code_delta:
import Mathlib.Data.Finset.Basic

variable {X Y : Type} [DecidableEq X] [DecidableEq Y] [DecidableEq (X × Y)]

def FoolingSet (Out : X × Y → Bool) (S : Finset (X × Y)) : Prop :=
  (∀ p ∈ S, Out p = true) /\
  (∀ p1 p2 ∈ S, p1.1 ≠ p2.1 → Out (p1.1, p2.2) = false \/ Out (p2.1, p1.2) = false)

variable {CrossSeq : Type} (CS : X × Y → CrossSeq) (Out : X × Y → Bool)

def CutAndPaste : Prop :=
  ∀ x1 y1 x2 y2, CS (x1, y1) = CS (x2, y2) → Out (x1, y2) = Out (x1, y1)

theorem fooling_set_implies_CS_injective_on_S (S : Finset (X × Y))
    (h_fool : FoolingSet Out S) (h_cut : CutAndPaste CS Out)
    (h_diag : ∀ p1 p2 ∈ S, p1.1 = p2.1 → p1 = p2) :
    ∀ p1 p2 ∈ S, CS p1 = CS p2 → p1 = p2 := by
  intro p1 hp1 p2 hp2 hCS
  by_contra h_neq
  have h_xy_neq : p1.1 ≠ p2.1 := by
    by_contra h_eq
    exact h_neq (h_diag p1 hp1 p2 hp2 h_eq)
  have h_false_cond := h_fool.2 p1 hp1 p2 hp2 h_xy_neq
  cases h_false_cond with
  | inl hf =>
      have h_out_true := h_fool.1 p1 hp1
      rw [← h_cut p1.1 p1.2 p2.1 p2.2 hCS] at h_out_true
      rw [hf] at h_out_true
      contradiction
  | inr hf =>
      have h_out_true := h_fool.1 p2 hp2
      rw [← h_cut p2.1 p2.2 p1.1 p1.2 (hCS.symm)] at h_out_true
      rw [hf] at h_out_true
      contradiction
expected_verifier_state: Definitions `FoolingSet` and `CutAndPaste` added; theorem `fooling_set_implies_CS_injective_on_S` proven with the diagonal uniqueness hypothesis `h_diag`, establishing that the crossing sequence map CS is injective on any fooling set S.
